title: The File of Fatima - Episode 1: Illuminations Before Starting
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: In this opening episode, Sheikh al-Ghizzi introduces the **"File of Fatima"** as a collection of historical documents and truths that have been systematically concealed or "registered against an unknown person" for centuries. He argues that the "Perfect Religion" can only be represented by the Infallible Imams and that scholars have only relative authority. Using the Quranic "Law of Concurrence," he explains that those who justify past oppressions are partners in them. The episode moves into primary documentation from Sunni "Sahihs" and historical texts, revealing Imam Ali's view of the early Caliphs as "liars and treacherous," and documenting the specific threat and physical assault—including the breaking of Lady Fatima's rib and the miscarriage of her son, Mohsin.

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens with a spiritual salutation to Lady Fatima and her descendants. He explains that his intention is to condense the most essential points of this file so it can be translated into major languages for the global Shi'a youth. He clarifies that his goal is not to provoke, but to present "Truths and Documents" from the depths of history and Hadith books—both Shi'a and Sunni—concerning the tragedy and oppression faced by the daughter of the Prophet.

Peace be upon Fatima the chosen one and al-Murtada, her husband and the Infallibles from her children. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place her Shi'a and her lovers, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings.
**The File of Fatima - Episode 1: Illuminations before starting.** There are points I wished to point out before I deal with the papers of this file.

**The First Point:** I had wished that the episodes of this program would be long, so that I could search in it every small and large thing related to this subject, near or far. This was my intention at the beginning, in this manner and fashion. But it occurred to my mind that this program should be **translated into the most important languages** spoken by the majority of the Shi'a of the Ahl al-Bayt in the world. We will try to translate it into **Persian, Turkish, Urdu, and English**.

The generations of the Shi'a youth of the Ahl al-Bayt who live in this era where the crowding of life, with all its effects of civilization and the noise of technology and the problems that are renewed every day, makes many of them extremely distant from such truths and such meanings. Therefore, I will focus my talk in these episodes on the **most important matters** and on the requests that are the foundation of this file. So that the program comes in line with its new plan, to be a support for all those who speak these different languages. I will try to be brief, but it is a brevity that does not neglect or diminish the important demands around which the papers of this file revolve.

**The Second Point:** The talk in these episodes is **not a provocation to anyone**, nor out of a love for excitement or noise. It is a statement of **Truths**. These truths are present in the books of the Shi'a of Fatima, and in the books of others.

The File of Fatima: Papers from the depths of the books of Hadith and books of History. The File of Fatima: **Truths and Documents** in the depths of history upon which the voice of the oppressors or the forgery of the pens of historians and traditionists—from the "Worshippers of the Bride" and those who ate the crumbs of the tables of the Sultans—has prevailed. The heinousness of the crime was recorded against "an unknown person" (didda majhul). This is how history judged the oppression of Fatima: either denial, or skepticism, or forgery, or distortion. And in the best of cases, it is recorded against an unknown person so that the File of Fatima disappears into the world of oblivion and is placed in the "Archive of Negligence" where the dust of lack of loyalty accumulates.

Muhammad (blessings of Allah be upon him and his family), when the Seal of the Prophets stood in his noble mosque and preached to his companions, saying: "Allah has cursed whoever prevents the hired worker from his wage." Then he says: "And I am your hired worker."
Say: I do not ask you for any reward for it except love for the kinsfolk. [Quran 42:23]

And I ask myself, before I ask anyone else: Who is the person in this world who is **closest to the Messenger of Allah**? Regarding the kinship connection, in the simplest linguistic meanings, in the time of Muhammad and after his departure from this world? Is there anyone more closely related in kinship and more intense in flesh and connection than **Fatima**? Fatima is the daughter of Muhammad. Muhammad left the world and has no one in this world—no father, no mother, no son—except Fatima.
